source.description: Breach notification timelines compiled from federal and state regulations
source.note: IMPORTANT: This is reference guidance, not legal advice. Always verify current requirements with official sources.

rules.threshold: 500+ individuals triggers immediate HHS notification and media notice; under 500 can be reported annually
rules.penalties: Up to $1.5M per violation category per year; criminal penalties possible
rules.notes: Discovery date starts the 60-day clock. Business associates must notify covered entities within 60 days.

rules.notification_deadline: As soon as possible, no later than 30 days
rules.threshold: 500+ consumers in a state triggers state AG notification
rules.penalties: Up to $100,000 per violation; individuals up to $10,000 per violation
rules.notes: FTC Safeguards Rule amended 2023 to require notification. Must notify FTC as soon as possible, no later than 30 days.

rules.notification_deadline: No specific federal deadline; follow state law
rules.threshold: Any unauthorized disclosure of education records
rules.notes: FERPA itself has limited breach notification requirements. Institutions typically follow applicable state breach laws.

rules.threshold: Any unauthorized collection or disclosure of children's personal information
rules.penalties: Up to $50,120 per violation (adjusted for inflation)
rules.notes: Operators must have reasonable procedures to protect children's data. FTC enforces via consent decrees.

rules.notification_deadline: Without unreasonable delay (generally 72 hours to AG for 500+)
rules.regulator_name: California Attorney General / California Privacy Protection Agency
rules.threshold: 500+ California residents triggers AG and media notification
rules.penalties: $2,500 per unintentional violation, $7,500 per intentional violation; private right of action $100-$750 per consumer per incident
rules.notes: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.82 provides the notification requirements. CPRA expanded enforcement via CPPA.

rules.notification_deadline: 72 hours from determination of cybersecurity event
rules.regulator_name: New York Department of Financial Services
rules.threshold: Any cybersecurity event with reasonable likelihood of materially harming normal operations
rules.penalties: Up to $250,000 per violation or 1% of total banking assets; individual liability for senior officers
rules.notes: 23 NYCRR 500.17. Must also notify DFS of ransomware payments within 24 hours (2023 amendment).

rules.notification_deadline: Without unreasonable delay, no later than 60 days
rules.penalties: Up to $7,500 per violation; AG exclusive enforcement
rules.notes: Va. Code §18.2-186.6. Follows Virginia's existing breach notification statute.

rules.threshold: 500+ Colorado residents triggers AG and media notification
rules.penalties: Up to $20,000 per violation under CCPA; separate privacy penalties under CPA
rules.notes: C.R.S. §6-1-716. One of the shortest breach notification deadlines in the US.

rules.threshold: Any breach of personal information of CT residents
rules.penalties: Up to $5,000 per violation; AG exclusive enforcement
rules.notes: C.G.S. §36a-701b. Connecticut also has separate insurance data breach requirements.

rules.threshold: Any breach of personal information of Utah residents
rules.penalties: Up to $7,500 per violation; AG exclusive enforcement with 30-day cure period
rules.notes: Utah Code §13-44-202. AG must provide 30-day cure notice before enforcement action.
Tex. Bus. & Com. Code §521.053. Texas Data Privacy and Security Act (2024).

rules.notification_deadline: As soon as practicable and without unreasonable delay
rules.regulator_name: Massachusetts Attorney General and Director of Consumer Affairs
rules.threshold: Any breach of personal information of MA residents
rules.penalties: Up to $5,000 per violation; treble damages under consumer protection law
rules.notes: M.G.L. c. 93H. One of the strictest state data protection regimes with 201 CMR 17.00 safeguards.

rules.threshold: 500+ Illinois residents triggers AG and media notification
rules.penalties: Up to $50,000 per violation; BIPA violations up to $5,000 per intentional violation
rules.notes: 815 ILCS 530. Illinois also has BIPA (Biometric Information Privacy Act) with private right of action.

rules.notification_deadline: 72 hours for cyber incidents; 24 hours for ransom payments
rules.threshold: Covered cyber incidents experienced by covered entities in critical infrastructure sectors (16 PPD-21 sectors). Incident must cause substantial loss of confidentiality/integrity/availability, impact operational systems, or result from supply chain compromise.
rules.penalties: No direct civil penalties. Non-compliance may result in CISA subpoena and referral to Attorney General for federal court enforcement. Contempt proceedings possible for subpoena non-compliance.
rules.notes: 6 USC §§ 681-681g. Reports receive strong legal protections: FOIA exempt, cannot be used for regulatory enforcement against reporting entity, litigation privilege applies. Final implementing rule expected March 2026; obligations not yet enforceable until final rule effective date.
